---
title: "Creating a New Event"
sidebarTitle: "New Event"
description: "How to configure a new event in Vivenu from scratch, including event details, metadata, and ticket categories."
---

## Overview

The configuration of an event in Vivenu is a critical task for FKL Operations. If events are configured incorrectly, various tools and platforms developed to support operations will break.

<Warning>
  Even if events are subsequently corrected, any ticket sales that have already occurred will be incorrect — it is not possible to re-trigger sold tickets when making changes.
</Warning>

The following must be configured correctly:

- Event naming convention
- Event metadata
- Ticket category naming convention
- Ticket category pricing
- Ticket category metadata

---

## Creating the Event

<Steps>
  <Step title="Select the hub">
    Navigate to the Vivenu dashboard and select the hub you wish to create an event for.

    <video controls>
      <source src="/videos/software/vivenu/new-event-list.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    </video>
  </Step>
  <Step title="Create a new event">
    Navigate to **Events** and click **New event**.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Populate the event details">
    On the sidebar, populate the following fields:

    | Field | Value |
    |--|--|
    | Photo | One of the existing configured photos |
    | Type | Single event (most common) |
    | Name | Taken directly from the event creation request form |
    | Time zone | **Set this first** — changing it after setting times will require the times to be re-entered |
    | Event start | 07:00 on the day of the event |
    | Event end | 18:00 on the day of the event |
    | Location name | Must match exactly from the tracks database |
    | Location street | Must match exactly from the tracks database |
    | Location ZIP code | Must match exactly from the tracks database |
    | Location city | Must match exactly from the tracks database |
    | Location country | Must match exactly from the tracks database |
    | Sales start | Current day |
    | Sales end | Day of the event at 08:00 |
    | Maximum amount of tickets | 120 |
    | Maximum tickets per order | 120 |
  </Step>
  <Step title="Create the event">
    Click **Create event**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

---

## Setting Event Information

<Steps>
  <Step title="Navigate to Information">
    Open the event and navigate to **Information**.

    <video controls>
      <source src="/videos/software/vivenu/new-event-information.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    </video>
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add the event slogan">
    Copy the relevant event slogan from the Event Slogan for Vivenu Events page and paste it into the **Event slogan** field.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add the event description">
    Copy the relevant event description from the Event Description for Vivenu Events page and paste it into the **Description** field.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Disable show start time">
    Disable the **Show start time** field.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Validate and save">
    Ensure all other details added during the Create stage are correct, then click **Save**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

---

## Setting Event Metadata

<Steps>
  <Step title="Navigate to Metadata">
    Navigate to **Custom** and scroll to the bottom of the page to the **Metadata** section.

    <video controls>
      <source src="/videos/software/vivenu/new-event-metadata.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    </video>
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add metadata fields">
    Click **Add field** and add the following keys. Note that all keys are **case-sensitive**.

    **`Type`** — set to one of the following values depending on the event:

    - `Testing`
    - `Regional`
    - `Pro`

    **`IsChampionship`** — set to `true`.

    <Note>
      `IsChampionship` is only required for Season Pass events.
    </Note>
  </Step>
</Steps>

---

## Creating Ticket Categories

<Warning>
  All tickets must have two metadata fields — `eligibleClasses` and `eligibleParts` — and both are case-sensitive. Pay careful attention when configuring these.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Add a new category">
    Navigate to **Ticket categories** and click **More categories**.

    <video controls>
      <source src="/videos/software/vivenu/new-event-ticket-categories.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    </video>
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set the category and ticket name">
    Under **Category name** and **Ticket name**, enter the name as defined in the specification sheet.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set the volume">
    Under **Category volume** and **Ticket type volume**, enter the volume as defined in the specification sheet.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set the price">
    Under **Price**, enter the price as defined in the specification sheet.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set tickets per order">
    Set the maximum number of tickets per order to **1**.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Open the ticket metadata">
    On the ticket card, click **...** to open the sidebar and scroll to the bottom to find the metadata section.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add eligibleClasses">
    Add the `eligibleClasses` field. Where multiple classes are needed, separate them with a comma. Valid values are:

    `BB`, `BR2R`, `CADL`, `CADH`, `CR2R`, `JRL`, `JRH`, `JR2R`
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add eligibleParts">
    Add the `eligibleParts` field. Valid values are:

    `D1/AM`, `D1/PM`, `D1/RACE`, `D1/RACE_AM`, `D1/RACE_PM`, `D2/AM`, `D2/PM`, `D2/RACE`, `D2/RACE_AM`, `D2/RACE_PM`
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

---

## Checking Default Settings

<Steps>
  <Step title="Disable Show other events">
    Navigate to **Display** and disable **Show other events**.

    <video controls>
      <source src="/videos/software/vivenu/new-event-display.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    </video>

    <Note>
      If you do not want customers to see the event yet, you can also enable **Hide** under Display.
    </Note>
  </Step>
  <Step title="Clear the confirmation page URL">
    Navigate to **Custom**, then **Order process**, and ensure the **Custom confirmation page URL** field is empty.
  </Step>
</Steps>
